title: Contents and Procedures for Filing Complaints under the 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs
content:
The complaint must contain the following formal requirements:
1. Name(s) and address(es) of the complainant(s)
2. Name(s) and address(es) of the respondent(s)
3. Specific act(s) or omission(s) constituting the alleged offense
4. Place where the offense was committed
5. Date when the offense was committed
6. The amount of claim, if any
7. The relief sought

The complaint must be in writing and under oath.

The Administration may initiate recruitment violation and disciplinary action cases on its own initiative based on official reports, especially from labor attaches and ambassadors worldwide, regarding violations of the Labor Code, POEA Rules and Regulations, and other issuances on overseas employment.

All recruitment violation and disciplinary action cases must be filed with the Administration within three (3) years from the time the cause of action accrued, happened, or transpired.
